commit.message:
add Blender FVMA mesh exporter with skeletal bone weights

New export_fvma.py Blender addon exports mesh geometry to the binary
FVMA format consumed by the engine's mesh_vao_deserialize() and
skeletal_mesh_create_from_fvma() loaders.

Features:
- Positions, normals, tangents, UV0/UV1, vertex colors
- Triangle fan tessellation for n-gon polygons
- Vertex deduplication by per-loop attribute combo
- Blender Z-up to engine Y-up coordinate conversion
- Skeletal bone weights/indices from Armature modifier
  (up to 4 influences per vertex, normalized)
- Inverse bind matrices (column-major, coordinate-converted)
- Bone ordering matches armature.bones traversal (same as fskel)
- Material index mapped to polygroup IDs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*

> <issue_title>Modernize tests by utilizing t.Setenv()</issue_title>
> <issue_description>### Background
> In `manael_test.go`, the test logic depends on environment variables
like `MANAEL_ENABLE_AVIF`. Previously, modifying environment variables
in tests required manual setup and teardown (`os.Setenv` and
`os.Unsetenv`).
> 
> Go 1.17 introduced `t.Setenv()`, which sets an environment variable
for the duration of the test and automatically restores it to its
original value when the test completes. This prevents environment
variable leakage between tests and simplifies the testing code.
> 
> ### Proposal
> Refactor the testing code in `manael_test.go` to use `t.Setenv()`
instead of relying on external environment configurations.
> 
> ### Example Implementation
> ```go
> func TestNewServeProxy_avif(t *testing.T) {
> 	// Automatically cleans up after the test completes
> 	t.Setenv("MANAEL_ENABLE_AVIF", "true")
>

commit.message:
Fix debug-build workflow Docker bind mount and PR checkout (#77)

* Initial plan

* Add --add-host flag support to container_args with tests and docs

Co-authored-by: ElvenSpellmaker <2286713+ElvenSpellmaker@users.noreply.github.com>

* Simplify host validation logic based on code review feedback

Co-authored-by: ElvenSpellmaker <2286713+ElvenSpellmaker@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix Docker-in-Docker bind mount path for GitHub Actions containers

When running eirctl build inside a GitHub Actions container (e.g., debug-build workflow),
the bind mount fails because the working directory path inside the container doesn't exist
on the actual Docker host.

This fix detects when running in GitHub Actions (via GITHUB_WORKSPACE env var) and uses
that for the bind mount source path instead of the container-internal working directory.

The GITHUB_WORKSPACE variable contains the actual host path even when the job runs in a
container, allowing Docker-in-Docker mounts to work correctly.

Fixes: Error response from daemon: invalid mount config for type "bind": bind source path does not exist

Co-authored-by: ElvenSpellmaker <2286713+ElvenSpellmaker@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix debug-build workflow to run directly on runner instead of in container

The debug-build.yml workflow was running the job inside a golang container,
which causes Docker-in-Docker bind mount failures. When the job runs in a
container, the working directory path (/__w/eirctl/eirctl) only exists inside
that container and doesn't exist on the actual host, causing Docker API bind
mounts to fail.

This change makes debug-build.yml match release.yml by:
- Removing the container: section (run directly on ubuntu runner)
- Adding Set up Go step to install Go on the runner

Now the job runs directly on the ubuntu-24.04 runner where Docker-in-Docker
bind mounts work correctly because paths are on the actual host filesystem.

Fixes: Error response from daemon: invalid mount config for type "bind": bind source path does not exist: /__w/eirctl/eirctl

Co-authored-by: ElvenSpellmaker <2286713+ElvenSpellmaker@users.noreply.github.com>

* Revert "Fix Docker-in-Docker bind mount path for GitHub Actions containers"

This reverts commit 70ba6043eb67cdcc0af6f90933d749a8bcd3df38.

* Fix debug-build to checkout PR branch instead of main

When triggered by /build-debug comment on a PR, the workflow was checking out
the main branch code instead of the PR branch code. This meant it was using
the old workflow configuration (with container) instead of the fixed version.

Added steps to:
1. Get the PR's head ref using GitHub API
2. Pass that ref to both checkout actions
3. Share the ref between jobs via outputs

Now when /build-debug is commented, it will checkout and build the actual PR's code.

Co-authored-by: ElvenSpellmaker <2286713+ElvenSpellmaker@users.noreply.github.com>

clean up env var parsing in a single place

commit.message:
Improve github-issues skill: fix MCP tools, add search reference, fix sub-issues docs (#888)

* Add advanced search reference with query syntax guide

Covers search qualifiers, boolean logic, date ranges, missing metadata
filters, common patterns, and when to use search vs list_issues.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add issue fields search support to search and issue-fields references

- Add Advanced Search Mode section to search.md covering field: qualifier,
  has:field: syntax, REST advanced_search=true, and GraphQL ISSUE_ADVANCED
- Add Searching by Field Values section to issue-fields.md with REST/GraphQL
  examples and qualifier reference table
- Note MCP search_issues limitation (no advanced_search support)
- Update SKILL.md capability table to mention issue field filters

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Clarify three search approaches: list_issues vs search_issues vs advanced search

Replace the simple two-column comparison with a capability matrix showing
what each approach supports (field filters, boolean logic, scope, etc.)
and a decision guide for when to use each one.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix issue field search syntax: use dot notation (field.name:value)

The colon notation (field:Name:Value) is silently ignored by the API.
The correct syntax is dot notation (field.priority:P0) which works in
REST (advanced_search=true), GraphQL (ISSUE_ADVANCED), and web UI.

Also supports has:field.name, no:field.name, and date comparisons.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix github-issues skill: correct MCP tools and add gh api workflows

The skill listed 5 MCP tools that don't exist (create_issue, update_issue,
get_issue, add_issue_comment, list_issue_types), causing tool-not-found
errors when agents tried to follow the skill instructions.

Changes:
- Split tools table into MCP (read ops) and CLI/API (write ops)
- Add gh api commands for creating, updating, commenting on issues
- Document that gh issue create doesn't support --type flag
- Add GraphQL query for discovering issue types
- Remove redundant [Bug]/[Feature] title prefixes (use type param instead)
- Update examples to use gh api instead of non-existent MCP tools

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix sub-issues reference: add gh api examples and integer gotcha

The sub_issue_id parameter requires an integer but gh api -f sends strings,
causing 422 errors. Updated all REST examples to use --input with raw JSON.

Also added:
- Recommended two-step workflow (create issue, then link)
- Explicit warning about -f vs --input for integer params
- Proper gh api command syntax instead of raw HTTP notation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR review: fix search docs accuracy and broken link

- Fix misleading claim about full boolean logic support (implicit AND only)
- Remove sort:updated from query example (it's an API param, not a qualifier)
- Clarify -linked:pr comment to avoid confusion with authoring status
- Fix relative link in issue-fields.md (sibling file, not nested path)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Improve projects reference: scope warning, gh api examples, issue-side queries

From hands-on usage: hit INSUFFICIENT_SCOPES trying to update project
status because token had read:project but not project write scope.

Added:
- OAuth scope requirements table with gh auth refresh workaround
- How to find an issue's project item ID (query from issue side)
- All GraphQL examples now use gh api graphql (copy-paste ready)
- End-to-end example: set issue status to In Progress

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add images-in-issues reference: hosting methods, pitfalls, screenshots

Documents three approaches for embedding images in issue comments
via CLI: Contents API with github.com/raw/ URLs, browser drag-drop
for permanent user-attachments URLs, and gist hosting limitations.

Includes puppeteer screenshot recipe and comparison table.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update README.skills.md with images reference

Run npm run build to regenerate skill listing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix issue-fields search: add GraphQL bulk query, caveat search syntax

The field.name:value search qualifier is unreliable via API (returns
0 results even when matching issues exist). Added a recommended
GraphQL approach that fetches issues and filters by issueFieldValues
client-side. Documented the correct IssueFieldSingleSelectValue
schema (name, not value). Marked search qualifier syntax as
experimental with a reliability warning.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(github-issues): improve project discovery guidance

The projectsV2 query param does keyword search, not exact match,
and sorts by recency. For large orgs like github, common words like
'issue' return 400+ results and bury the target project.

Added a priority-ordered discovery strategy:
1. Direct lookup by number (instant)
2. Reverse lookup from a known issue's projectItems (most reliable)
3. GraphQL name search with client-side jq filtering (fallback)
4. MCP tool (small orgs only)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(state): remove fatal assertionFailure for detached nodes (#10)

`@State` getter/setter crash with `assertionFailure` when accessed after
the view's `Node` is deallocated. This is fatal in debug builds. Common
trigger: an async `.task(id:)` closure sets `@State` after the view is
removed from the hierarchy — the `weak var node` in `StateReference`
becomes `nil`, hitting the assertion before the guard's fallback path.

```
State.wrappedValue.setter + 688
  assertionFailure("Attempting to modify @State variable before view is instantiated")
```

The message is also misleading — this isn't "before instantiation," it's
after removal.

### Changes

- **`State.swift`**: Remove `assertionFailure` from both getter and
setter guards. The fallback behavior (return `initialValue` / no-op) is
already correct and matches SwiftUI semantics.
- **`StateTests.swift`**: Direct unit tests for `State` get/set when
`valueReference.node` is nil.

block itself needs the unsafe keyword, and since the function is
declared 'safe fn', the call doesn't need an unsafe block.

> <issue_title>Feature: Expose Prometheus metrics on a dedicated
internal port</issue_title>
> <issue_description>### Background
> Following the OpenTelemetry instrumentation (see previous issue), we
need a way to actually scrape and monitor these metrics in production.
Prometheus is the industry standard for this.
> However, exposing a `/metrics` endpoint on the main proxy port is
dangerous, as it risks hijacking legitimate upstream paths and exposing
sensitive internal metrics to the public internet.
> 
> ### Proposal
> Configure the OpenTelemetry Prometheus exporter and expose the
`/metrics` endpoint on a **completely separate, dedicated internal HTTP
port**. This metrics server should only start if explicitly enabled via
configuration.
> 
> ### Configuration
> - `MANAEL_METRICS_PORT`: The port on which to expose the metrics
server (e.g., `9090`). If not set or empty, the metrics server is
disabled.
> 
> ### Tasks
> - Add dependencies for `go.opentelemetry.io/otel/exporters/prometheus`
and `github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus/promhttp`.
> - Configure the OpenTelemetry MeterProvider to use the Prometheus
exporter.
> - If `MANAEL_METRICS_PORT` is set, start a new background goroutine
with an HTTP server listening on that port, serving `promhttp.Handler()`
at the `/metrics` path.
> 
> ### Example Implementation Idea
> ```go
> package main
> 
> import (
> 	"log"
> 	"net/http"
> 	"os"
> 	"go.opentelemetry.io/otel/exporters/prometheus"
> 	"go.opentelemetry.io/otel/sdk/metric"
> 	"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus/promhttp"
> )
> 
> func setupMetricsServer() {
> 	metricsPort := os.Getenv("MANAEL_METRICS_PORT")
> 	if metricsPort == "" {
> 		return // Metrics server disabled
> 	}
> 
> 	// Setup Prometheus exporter
> 	exporter, err := prometheus.New()
> 	if err != nil {
> 		log.Fatalf("failed to initialize prometheus exporter: %v", err)
> 	}
> 	provider := metric.NewMeterProvider(metric.WithReader(exporter))
> 	// Set global MeterProvider...
> 
> 	go func() {
> 		metricsMux := http.NewServeMux()
> 		metricsMux.Handle("/metrics", promhttp.Handler())
> 		log.Printf("Starting metrics server on :%s", metricsPort)
> if err := http.ListenAndServe(":"+metricsPort, metricsMux); err != nil
{
> 			log.Printf("Metrics server error: %v", err)
> 		}
> 	}()
> }

commit.message:
Fix opaque "Edge Function returned a non-2xx status code" error on user update (#80)

* Initial plan

* Fix updateUser edge function error: improve error extraction and add robustness guards

- userService.js: Replace single-field JSON extraction with extractFunctionError()
  helper that reads the response body as text once, then tries JSON.parse to check
  both the `error` field (used by the edge function) and the `message` field (used
  by the Supabase gateway/relay). Falls back to the raw text if the body is not
  JSON. This ensures the actual failure reason is shown instead of the opaque
  "Edge Function returned a non-2xx status code" SDK fallback. Applied to
  createUser, deleteUser, and updateUser.

- admin-user-management/index.ts: Add step-0 guard that returns a descriptive
  500 error when SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY is missing, rather than letting
  createClient() throw inside the try-catch (which would previously surface as
  the generic "Internal server error"). Also add a guard preventing admins from
  deactivating their own account (which would lock them out of the admin panel
  on the next page load).
